message:
🐛 Fix broken contains operator in dynamic cluster group evaluation (#2622)

* 🐛 Fix broken contains operator in dynamic cluster group evaluation

The contains operator was checking string length instead of substring
membership, causing clusters like "prod-eu" to incorrectly match
filters like contains("dev").

Fixes #2606

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* 🐛 Fix frontend TEXT_OPERATORS to send 'contains' operator to backend

The UI was mapping the "contains" label to the "eq" operator value,
making the backend contains fix unreachable. Now sends the correct
operator and adds a separate "equals" option for exact matching.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -3,6 +3,7 @@ package handlers
 import (
 	"context"
 	"log"
+	"strings"
 	"time"
 
 	"github.com/gofiber/fiber/v2"
@@ -577,7 +578,7 @@ func matchString(actual, operator, expected string) bool {
 	case "neq":
 		return actual != expected
 	case "contains":
-		return len(actual) > 0 && len(expected) > 0 && (actual == expected || len(actual) > len(expected))
+		return strings.Contains(actual, expected)
 	default:
 		return false
 	}

@@ -70,7 +70,8 @@ const FILTER_FIELDS = [
 ]
 
 const TEXT_OPERATORS = [
-  { value: 'eq', label: 'contains' },
+  { value: 'eq', label: 'equals' },
+  { value: 'contains', label: 'contains' },
   { value: 'neq', label: 'excludes' },
 ]
